subject: Social Science
learning_outcomes: Identify the main features of the climate of India
Analyse the climatic conditions of the seasons of India
Explain the distribution and effects of seasonal rainfall

key_terms.definition: Seasonal winds that bring rainfall to India from June to September
October-November season when monsoon withdraws from India, causing cyclones in Bay of Bengal
Pre-monsoon rainfall in West Bengal during April-May
Pre-monsoon rainfall in Karnataka during April-May
Pre-monsoon rainfall in Kerala during April-May
Pre-monsoon rainfall in Uttar Pradesh during April-May

mcqs.question: The eastern parts of western ghats get less rainfall than the western parts because
mcqs.explanation: Eastern parts of Western Ghats are rain shadow regions

mcqs.question: During the Retreating Monsoon season cyclones occur in the Bay of Bengal due to

options.A: Difference in temperature and pressure between land and ocean
options.C: These areas lie high from sea level
options.D: North Indian areas are far from sea

mcqs.explanation: Cyclones occur due to differences in temperature and pressure of land and sea

mcqs.question: The period of Retreating Monsoons season in India
mcqs.explanation: The Retreating Monsoon season is October-November in India

one_mark_questions.question: Which are the two branches of the southwest monsoon?
one_mark_questions.answer: Arabian Sea branch and Bay of Bengal branch

one_mark_questions.question: Which state does not receive rain during the southwest monsoon season?
Which place in India receives the highest rainfall?
Which region receives the lowest rainfall in India?
What are the main climatic seasons of India?
one_mark_questions.answer: Summer (March-May), Southwest Monsoon (June-September), Retreating Monsoon (October-November), Winter (December-February)

three_mark_questions.question: Indian agriculture is a gambling with the monsoon. Justify.
answer: The main occupation of the people of India is agriculture
Agriculture is controlled by the monsoon winds
Failure of rainfall leads to drought
Heavy rainfall causes loss of life and damage to property
Hence Indian agriculture is called a gamble with the monsoons
